title: The Winners and Losers of the 2026 Golden Globes
text: Hamnet emerged as a significant contender, winning Best Motion Picture, Drama. Jessie Buckley claimed Best Actress in a Motion Picture, Drama for her performance in the film. The Secret Agent, a political thriller in the non-English language category, won Best Motion Picture, Non-English Language. Wagner Moura upset other competitors to win Best Actor in a Motion Picture, Drama. However, Sinners failed to gain traction in the drama categories despite early awards season momentum. Though it secured two recognition awards, it came up empty where it mattered most, including Best Motion Picture, Drama and Best Actor in a Motion Picture, Drama. Rhea Seehorn's performance in Pluribus earned her the Golden Globe for Best Actress in a Television Series, Drama. The article notes this represents significant vindication for Seehorn, whose acclaimed work in Better Call Saul had been largely overlooked by major awards bodies. A concerning development noted in the ceremony was that Best Original Score was presented during a commercial break, diminishing recognition for composers' contributions to award-winning films.
